description: Non-latency metadata for every hosting provider evaluated in the MCP server latency benchmark. Covers providers that are in the benchmark, providers not yet added, and providers explicitly excluded. Updated manually as new providers are evaluated.

criteria_explained.sources: List of URLs where the information about this provider was found. Each entry includes the URL and a short description of what was found there.
criteria_explained.benchmark_status: Whether this provider is currently measured in the benchmark ('in_benchmark'), evaluated but not yet deployed ('pending'), or excluded with a reason ('excluded').
criteria_explained.credit_card_required: Whether a credit card is required to create an account and deploy a server. 'account_only' means no CC needed to create the account but CC is needed to deploy. 'yes' means CC is required at some point before a server can run.
criteria_explained.agent_native_deployment: Whether the full deployment process (excluding account creation) can be performed by an agent from the terminal, without any manual action on a web interface. 'yes' means a CLI covers everything. 'partial' means some steps require browser interaction. 'no' means deployment requires the browser.
criteria_explained.free_tier_type: Type of free offering: 'permanent' (free forever within usage limits), 'trial' (free for a limited time or resource budget, then requires payment), 'none' (no free option).
criteria_explained.free_tier_limit: The specific limit of the free offering, if any.
criteria_explained.agent_native_score: Overall assessment of how well this provider fits an agent-native workflow where an agent handles deployment end-to-end without human intervention or payment.

providers.name: Cloudflare Workers
providers.agent_native_score: excellent — no CC, permanent free tier, full CLI deployment

sources.content: Free tier limits: 100,000 requests per day

providers.name: Vercel
providers.free_tier_limit: unknown — request limits not publicly documented in detail
providers.agent_native_score: good — no CC, CLI available, free tier permanent

providers.name: Netlify
providers.agent_native_score: good — no CC, CLI available, free tier permanent

sources.content: Free tier: 125k serverless function invocations per month

providers.name: Railway
providers.free_tier_limit: $5 of credit per month on the Hobby plan
providers.agent_native_score: unknown — CC requirement to be verified

providers.name: Supabase Edge Functions
providers.agent_native_score: unknown — CC requirement to be verified

sources.content: Free tier: 500,000 Edge Function invocations per month

providers.name: Fermyon
providers.agent_native_score: unknown — free tier and CC requirement to be verified

providers.name: Val Town
providers.agent_native_score: poor — no CLI, deployment required manual copy-paste on the Val Town web interface
providers.notes: At time of deployment, Val Town had no CLI. The server code had to be pasted manually into the Val Town web editor. This blocks fully agent-native workflows.

providers.name: Render
providers.deployment_cli: none — deploys via GitHub repo connection
providers.free_tier_limit: Free instance sleeps after 15 minutes of inactivity — cold start on next request
providers.agent_native_score: partial — no CLI, deploy is triggered by pushing to a connected GitHub repo (agent can do this), but initial setup requires web interface

sources.content: Free tier: web services sleep after 15 minutes of inactivity

providers.notes: The free tier instance sleeps after inactivity. This means latency measurements on Render free tier include cold start time — relevant to our ping frequency analysis.

providers.name: Fly.io
providers.exclusion_reason: No permanent free tier. Fly.io offers a 2-hour or 7-day trial only, after which a credit card is required to run any app. A benchmark that requires paid infrastructure cannot be reproduced freely by the community.
providers.credit_card_required: yes — required to deploy any app beyond the 2h/7-day trial
providers.free_tier_limit: 2 hours of machine runtime or 7 days, whichever comes first
providers.agent_native_score: CLI excellent but CC is a hard blocker — requires human intervention to add payment before any deployment

sources.content: Free trial details: 2 hours of machine runtime or 7 days, whichever comes first. Apps stop running after the trial ends.
List of available regions and compute pricing

providers.notes: The fly CLI is one of the best available for agent-native deployments. The only blocker is the payment requirement. Fly.io would be ideal for pingers across multiple regions if cost were not a constraint.

providers.name: Google Cloud Run
providers.notes: gcloud CLI not installed on the deployment machine. To be deployed manually or after CLI installation.

providers.name: Azure Container Apps
providers.notes: az CLI not installed on the deployment machine. To be deployed manually or after CLI installation.

providers.name: Replit
providers.exclusion_reason: Free plan published apps go offline automatically after 30 days. A benchmark requires servers that stay up permanently. Keeping a Replit app running beyond 30 days requires a paid plan (Core at $18/month).
providers.credit_card_required: no for account creation, yes to keep a server running beyond 30 days
providers.deployment_cli: none — deploys via Replit web interface only
providers.free_tier_limit: 1 published app, automatically goes offline after 30 days. 2GB workspace storage. Daily Agent credits (monthly cap). Monthly credits for databases, object storage, AI integrations.
providers.agent_native_score: poor — no CLI, browser-only deployment, 30-day limit on free published apps

sources.content: Pricing comparison page — does NOT mention the 30-day limit on free published apps
Starter plan technical documentation — discloses that free published apps go offline after 30 days

providers.notes: WARNING: The 30-day limit is not mentioned anywhere on the pricing comparison page (https://replit.com/pricing). It is only disclosed in the technical documentation. A developer who deploys on the free plan will not discover this limit until their app goes offline.

providers.name: Alpic

sources.content: Official Alpic MCP server template — uses skybridge framework and alpic deploy CLI
OpenAI MCP deployment guide — recommends Alpic as a hosting provider

providers.notes: Alpic has its own CLI (alpic deploy) and runtime (skybridge). Uses TypeScript, not raw Node.js HTTP. CLI not installed on the deployment machine.
